case_record_identity: case_number is THE identity (mint), surfaced as the CASE-<n> Registry synonym (CASE-481)
version_contract: bundle_digest (auto — computed by the /manifest endpoint over the served files) is the currency signal: a fetcher re-fetches when it changes. files[] + bundle_digest are derived from the kb-client/ directory at serve time, so the served manifest can never drift from the actual bundle. client_version is an informational human semver only (use bundle_digest for currency). (CASE-437.)
note: v3 (CASE-481/482), the (A) design: ONE write path. The gateway has a single write endpoint, POST {BASE_PATH}/server-api/kb/write/:type ({data, edges?} to create/mint/upsert, or {patch, match} to partial-update), and parses no files. Per-type write behaviour is data, not code — WRITE_POLICY documents drive mint-vs-natural, the synonym shape, and per-parent scopes (e.g. CASE_RESPONSE -> CASE-<n>#<seq>). Identity: mint types carry a KB-allocated number + synonym; natural types upsert by identity_fields. No identity_fields:[] loophole. The client: kb-write.py is THE write client for every type — it parses + validates the source (file/dir/--json, plus git-stats computed via --git-repo) and posts to /write/:type; case-fetch.py is the read client (case/journey/list/fireside); kb_client_core.py is the shared transport core (kb.json/key resolution + gw_get/gw_post with failover). Reads and writes both go through the gateway — never the document-store backend. files[] + bundle_digest are derived at serve time (not stored here).
